sources.notes: Choose an order to defeat monsters/rooms with parameters A,B,C.
Official tutorial proves all C>0 events can be processed by increasing B, using the smallest feasible A.
Maspy notes C=0 items should be postponed, then the same greedy is applied after C>0 items.

annotation.summary: Determine whether all dungeon events can be completed in a valid order.
annotation.constraints: n is large enough that sorting plus logarithmic multiset operations is intended.
annotation.core_idea: Events with C=0 never help earlier, so postpone them. For C>0, process events by increasing B; if two such events are out of B order, swapping them is never worse. Among currently feasible A values, always use the smallest one, preserving larger A for later harder requirements. After all helpful events are handled, process the remaining C=0 events with the same smallest-feasible greedy.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) per test case.
tricks: sort by B via exchange argument

solution_variants.name: sorted-greedy-multiset
solution_variants.summary: Sort positive-gain events by B and repeatedly assign the smallest feasible A using a multiset.

tags.evidence: The proof relies on sorting by B and always choosing the smallest feasible resource.
C>0 events are processed in increasing B order.
A multiset or ordered structure is used to find the smallest feasible A.
